pillars.name: Lower-body power
pillars.what: Explosive force the legs produce across repeated efforts — the power football demands in repeated sprints and jumps.

pillars.name: Speed & acceleration
pillars.what: How quickly Arjun accelerates from a standing start and carries that speed over distance.

pillars.name: Agility & change of direction
pillars.what: How sharply he changes direction, shuffles, and recovers — every turn and reaction on the pitch.

pillars.name: Ball coordination
pillars.what: How accurately he strikes a lofted long pass to a target — consistency across five attempts, not one good kick.

pillars.name: Endurance
pillars.what: How he sustains effort over a continuous run — one of several stamina markers, directionally useful.

pillars.name: Mobility & flexibility
pillars.what: How freely he moves through a full range — supporting comfortable movement and athletic demand.

pillars.name: Strength
pillars.what: How his muscles sustain effort — strength-endurance, distinct from maximal strength, which we don't measure.
pillars.flag: plank 3:00 — held to the test limit

narrative.text: Arjun completed all seven tests on Combine Day. He covered the 800 metres at a steady pace, held his plank to the full test limit, and gave consistent effort at every station.
narrative.attribution: Observed by the coaching team · Tekkers Football Academy

next_line: Arjun's next Fitness Snapshot will be ready at the end of Month 3, when this cycle of training completes.

page2.lead: This is a snapshot of Arjun's physical state on the day we measured — power, speed, agility, ball coordination, endurance, mobility, and strength. The numbers are facts, not judgements. This card is not a medical or diagnostic tool, and not a comparison against any benchmark, age norm, or peer.

blocks.title: What we measured
blocks.body: Seven movement tests run on Combine Day, each a real measurement of one capacity. We show the numbers honestly, with no score and no rating.

blocks.title: How often
blocks.body: Twice in a 3-month cycle — end of Month 1 and end of Month 3. Both follow the same protocol, so a change between them is meaningful.

blocks.title: A note on how some tests are recorded
blocks.body: Some carry a little context so the picture stays honest — the plank is held only to a 3:00 limit, and the long-pass distance is set per session and shown beside the result. These describe how the test was done, not marks against the athlete.

blocks.title: If Arjun trained with us earlier
blocks.body: Several of these tests — the triple hop, T-test, sit-and-reach, push-ups, plank, and 800 m — are identical across age groups, so the numbers tell a story over multiple years, not just one cycle.

blocks.title: A note on the numbers
blocks.body: We deliberately do not compare Arjun against other athletes, age-band averages, or any benchmark, and we use no traffic-light scoring. The numbers describe what happened on the day. We collected only height, weight, and the seven measurements; the data is held by Maidan Play and not shared outside the academy. If you'd like to discuss anything here, the coaching and curriculum team is available.
